description_long: This is a compact hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural brown finish and a visible grain pattern. The handle is capped with a red-brown grip section near the head, likely for improved handling and comfort. The axe head is made of metallic gray steel, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll opposite the cutting edge. The blade has a single sharp edge and a slightly flared beard, suitable for chopping or splitting wood. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with the top of the handle visible through the eye of the axe. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A compact hand axe with a curved wooden handle, red-brown grip, and a broad steel blade with a single sharp edge.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle, red-brown grip, and the broad steel blade with its sharp edge.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, showing the rectangular cross-section and the width of the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the handle's curvature, grip section, and the blade's wedge profile.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the handle's end grain and the thickness of the blade edge.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a rectangular cross-section, natural grain, and a red-brown grip section near the head for improved hold.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a broad cutting edge and a flat poll; slight beard present, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle passing through the eye of the blade head.
